body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: As a parent, I find the School of Communication's communication regarding my child's academic progress to be commendable and genuinely reassuring. Semester progress reports are issued promptly through the parent portal, and attendance alerts are sent in real time, allowing me to stay informed and intervene early when needed. I particularly value the parent information sessions organized each semester, where faculty and administrators present academic expectations, available support services, and the student performance outlook in detail. The level of transparency and proactive communication from the Academic Affairs Office has greatly increased my confidence in the institution's commitment to student success and parental partnership.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: I have observed several concerning aspects of teaching quality in my Media Studies courses at the School of Communication this semester that I feel compelled to bring to attention. Certain lectures are delivered without adequate structure, making it difficult to follow the logical progression of content, and some lecturers rely heavily on reading directly from slides rather than elaborating or encouraging discussion. Assessment criteria are sometimes communicated after assignments are already underway, which creates unnecessary confusion and unfair evaluation conditions. I strongly raise concerns about the institution to invest in faculty development programs focused on modern pedagogy, student engagement techniques, and constructive feedback practices to address these shortcomings urgently.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Communication has been superb and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I commend the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: Campus safety standards at the School of Communication are reasonable, providing an adequate baseline of physical security and student welfare programming while leaving meaningful room for improvement in several critical areas. Core security measures — including campus access controls, security patrols, and emergency contact systems — are functional and reasonably communicated. However, mental health and counseling resources are stretched and insufficiently staffed relative to the student population, creating unacceptable wait times during periods of peak demand. I would strongly encourage the Student Affairs Office to expand counseling capacity, improve lighting and surveillance in underserved campus areas, and develop a more proactive student welfare outreach program for at-risk individuals.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: I have experienced inconsistent financial management from the Finance Department at the School of Communication that has caused considerable stress and inconvenience to our family over the course of this academic year. Scholarship disbursements were repeatedly delayed without advance notification, and when I contacted the Finance Department for clarification, responses were slow and sometimes contradictory between staff members. Unexpected additional charges appeared on the fee statement mid-semester without prior written notice, which I find both financially disruptive and contrary to good institutional governance practices. I formally request that the Finance Department implement automated disbursement notifications, enforce accurate billing practices, and establish a clear parent-facing communication protocol for all financial matters.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal provided by the School of Communication, supported by the IT Support Department, is commendable in its design, functionality, and reliability, and has become an invaluable tool for staying connected to my child's academic journey throughout the year. The interface is intuitive and well-organized, presenting grade information, attendance records, fee statements, and university announcements in a clear and accessible format that requires no technical expertise. The system is reliably available, with minimal downtime, and notifications are pushed promptly for important academic events and institutional announcements. I appreciate the university's investment in digital parent engagement tools, which I believe genuinely strengthen the home-university partnership and support better student outcomes.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling at the School of Communication is reasonable, covering essential advising needs without providing the depth and personalization that would truly distinguish the service. My child has found their assigned advisor to be approachable and knowledgeable on the whole, though appointment availability is sometimes limited during peak periods of the academic calendar. The counseling provided tends to be reactive — responding to student-initiated queries rather than proactively identifying and addressing potential challenges before they escalate. A more structured check-in model, combined with a larger and better-trained advising team, would significantly improve the consistency and impact of academic counseling across the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Communication, managed by the Student Affairs Office, are insufficient and fall significantly short of what students need to manage the academic, personal, and financial challenges of university life. Mental health support in particular is severely limited — appointment wait times can exceed three weeks, which is unacceptable for students experiencing acute stress. Financial hardship assistance processes are slow, bureaucratic, and poorly communicated, leaving vulnerable students without timely help during critical periods. The university must urgently expand its counseling capacity, simplify hardship application procedures, and raise awareness of available services through more proactive and visible outreach to the student community.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: The physical facilities at the School of Communication, under the responsibility of the Facilities Management, present inconsistent conditions that negatively impact the daily learning experience of students. Several lecture halls have malfunctioning air conditioning systems that make studying in warm months genuinely uncomfortable and distracting. Toilet facilities in older buildings are poorly maintained and frequently reported without timely resolution from the facilities team. Study spaces are chronically insufficient relative to the student population, particularly during examination periods when demand peaks significantly. I urge the university to prioritize facilities maintenance budgets, establish a transparent issue-reporting system, and expand study space capacity as part of its next capital improvement plan.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: As a parent who has closely observed my child's experience at the School of Communication over the past year, I can say with genuine satisfaction that the institution has provided an superb educational and personal development environment that has exceeded our family's expectations in most meaningful respects. The combination of academic rigor, caring faculty, comprehensive student support, and strong career preparation has resulted in visible and meaningful growth in my child's competence, confidence, and professional readiness. I would enthusiastically recommend this university to other families seeking a high-quality, student-centered tertiary education for their children, and I look forward to continuing to partner with the institution as my child completes the remainder of their degree.
